title: How do I remove branding on emails/forms?
excerpt:
Hi, 
How can I remove monday’s branding from outbound emails through the Gmail integration?

posts.author: Patrick (patrick@parvenunext.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

How can I remove monday’s branding from outbound emails through the Gmail integration?

image842×812 28.8 KB

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
This was a similar post from last month and @Galit-monday.com said she’d flag it with the Products Team.

Remove Monday.com branding from emails generated through the Gmail or Outlook integrations Feature requests
Use case We use the email integration to send automatic email updates to our clients (we are a marketing agency) about the status of a pulse – if we’ve received the request, it’s in progress, been completed. [24%20PM] Pain point We see there is “Powered by Monday.com” branding at the bottom of these emails. [2019-06-05_14-41-50] This does not look good and is confusing to our clients, as the support was provided by our agency, not Monday.com. Additionally, we are not using Monday.com for free…

posts.author: Patrick (patrick@parvenunext.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, this isn’t a feature I’d use until it’s gone. I’m trying to sell our products, not monday’s.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this.
I add to this feature, the possibility to customize the EMAIL SUBJECT in any notification,
actually displays (Monday.Com), i think for our customers this should be in first place the company name, then monday.com.-

posts.author: Imann (Alexander)
posts.cookedHtml:
Well, this feature is very raw. We need a fully customizable emails, with templates both for subject and body.
Hope we will be heard by Monday)

posts.author: Lisa-monday.com (Lisa Ceccato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey!

Lisa here. I can definitely let you know that I hear you. I’ve pinged the developers to learn more about what can be done with the branding I will follow-up on this chain with an update.

Lisa

posts.author: mtvanc (MT)
posts.cookedHtml: +1, without being able to remove the branding, this is useless

posts.author: Thean (Thean Oberhollenzer)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 ! This should absolutely be self-evident - same for the form view!

immagine.png726×499 12 KB

posts.author: alexm (Alex M)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote for this to be accelerated. It cheapens our brand image and I agree with the other posts - we pay our fees to use a premium product, and this branding doesn’t reflect that.

posts.author: Gretchen (Gretchen Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday – This post has a recent question from other Monday users as does my similar post (Remove Monday.com branding from emails generated through the Gmail or Outlook integrations)

Can we get an update from Monday.com as to the status of removing the branding?

Thank you

posts.author: GimmeRope (Jason Keenan)
posts.cookedHtml: Since we’re paying for a premium service, it would make sense to have the branding removed from forms

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
you can do this from the admin options easily… , you can add 2 logos, one for the email headers and one (like an icon) of 40x40 pixels.

problem is: monday have changed the logo that forms have, now is using the 40x40 px logo and not the email header (580x80 pixels) expanded logo…

this is a BIG problem, because our branding logo on forms is barely visible

i have reported this as an issue 30 days ago, and there are no news yet

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @GimmeRope

There’s already a discussion open on this topic so I’m going to move your request there!

Re the bug you mention @hlopezvc I’ll check to see the status.

Thanks both!

posts.author: GimmeRope (Jason Keenan)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this request (removing monday.com branding from forms)? Seems like it would be an easy fix… ?

posts.author: smc462 (Sarah C)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a critical update for forms; they are consumer-facing for many of us, and the clickable Monday branding is confusing for customers. Additionally, the default “thank you” page on the forms has an odd and confusing sales banner on the bottom that hurts the consumer experience when interacting with my business. Would love an update on this feature!

posts.author: IanFury (Ian Fury)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday.com branding should be removed from emails, exports to spreadsheets etc. except for anyone on a free trail. Once you are paying for the platform it is obvious that only your own branding should be on client facing material.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree, this is the reason for not to use forms externally, which is a shame.

posts.author: ChrisCollins (Chris Collins)
posts.cookedHtml: This is crazy - please remove your branding from our forms asap so we can use them.

posts.author: reinhardts (Reinhardt Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree with all of the above. We cannot use forms if there is monday.com branding, so I am stuck using a different method for entry now on our customer-facing inputs.

Please remove the branding and the sales batter after a submission.

[EDIT]
Contacted support, and they said I should make a feature request, so I’ve done that here: Remove monday.com branding from Forms

posts.author: anon34162818 (anon34162818)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree with concept for paid version removing all branding, it does confuse customers and we should be able to control whether or not a customer see the Monday.com logo.

It actually forces you to use other form options to avoid the forced branding.

I get the reasoning but I also think if you are paying you should be making the decision.

Chad

posts.author: Ioannis (Ioannis Papaioannou)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree. The branding is a show stopper for us as well.

posts.author: erics (Eric Siegert)
posts.cookedHtml: We are not using Software / Functions without White-Label Options. I would highly recommend to delete those logos if you want people to engage with those functions. Especially for sensitive industries - with regard to privacy etc. - it’s a No-Go to show off with logos from other companies.

posts.author: NotSoShabby (Refael)
posts.cookedHtml: This is indeed very uncommon and very bad value proposition in a premium product. Any serious brand will not allow a different brand to “ride” on its development by pushing its logo in its embedded code. Its understandable in a free product - but not in one that you pay a lot of money for.

posts.author: ErickE-monday.com (Erick E.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone!

Thank you for all the follow-ups and requests. The branding should no longer be visible on e-mails.

As for the forms, I checked with the department in charge of forms and it is something they are looking to analyze this quarter and depending on their findings move forward with a solution some time in the near future.

posts.author: reinhardts (Reinhardt Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the update! I really hope they move forward quickly with that. We are waiting to use the forms until that’s done.

posts.author: Sven.H (Sven Hausherr)
posts.cookedHtml: Hope they take out the branding for the paying customers from forms (and success message) soon

posts.author: ChrisCollins (Chris Collins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great news about the emails, thank you.

Has there been any update on the forms?

posts.author: ErickE-monday.com (Erick E.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Chris,

No update as of yet, but when I checked with the team, they mentioned it is something they are looking to analyze as a possibility this quarter or next, so we hope that this improvement may be made in the near future

posts.author: LloydBurger (Lloyd Andre Burger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I would love to start exporting forms as PDFs without monday.com branding.

There are just a few issues I have which makes the export quite useless.

When exporting the form the layout is fine but the way it looks in a PDF export is horrid and very unprofessional looking. It also exports the pdf as an image and not as data that you can edit afterwards. The image then is sliced to multiple bits on the PDF form which is separates it into pages.

I want to use this function for product quality complaints and send the exported form view as a report to the client that we have looked into the complaint and what we have done to solve it.

Does anyone have a work around for this? It would be greatly appreciated!

I tried using integrations with Jotform that can take data from monday.com and fill in a form automatically for me. It doesn’t seem like there is such functionality?

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey guys

We released the option to remove branding on Forms for Pro accounts and above!

Hope you enjoy
Thanks for sharing your feedback!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml: What is the probability that this will be extended to Excel exports?

posts.author: J-Dog (Jude Beggs)
posts.cookedHtml: how? I can’t find the option in settings…

posts.author: ErickE-monday.com (Erick E.)
posts.cookedHtml:
@J-Dog.

In Form View, you can access the view settings at the top right side of your page. You will then see the ability to remove this branding towards the bottom of the setting pane.

image544×1156 51.7 KB

posts.author: ErickE-monday.com (Erick E.)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell - I’d be happy to pass this request along to our product team for further consideration

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml: Also want the branding removed from Excel.

posts.author: gmmyingtao (Mingming Kellogg)
posts.cookedHtml:
@ErickE-monday.com Can you move Monday branding from quote? Same, can’t use this feature until it’s gone.

image676×698 24.3 KB

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
